Non aatem est atile nec homini freqaenter in aqaa babieare, nisi macer et aridas sit, qai facile infrigidatar et facile incalescit, qaod tenaes cames habet; et iste in aqaa balneet, ut aliquantum calorem corpori suo adhibeat et ut corpus suum humectet. Illi yero, qui pingues cames habet, balnea aquamm obsunt, quod interius calidi et humidi sunt, et ideo si corporibus suis maiorem calorem et humiditatem addunt, corpus eorum laedunt, nisi forte rarissime bahieis in aqua utantur, ut lutum tantum abluant et cito exeant.
text_translation: On the diversity of waters and baths. It is not advisable for a person to bathe frequently in water, unless they are weak and dry, who easily become chilled and easily heated, who have a tenacious body; and such a person bathes in water, so that they may add some warmth to their body and moisten their body. Those, however, who have a fat body, should avoid baths of water, which are internally hot and moist, and therefore if they add more heat and moisture to their bodies, they harm their bodies, unless they rarely use baths in water, so that they may only wash away the mud and quickly exit.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Aquae yero, quae bonae sunt ad potandum, bonae sunt ad bahiea; sed modice calefiant, et ita homo in eis dia sedere potest, si yult, quoniam inflmaitatem homini non inferunt sed bonum et pulchrum colorem ei dant. Sed aquae, quae malae sunt ad potandum, malae sunt etiam ad babiea. Quod si necesse fuerit, ut aliquis balnea in istis accipiat, fortiter coquantur, ut liyor, qui in eis est, per coctionem minuatur, et breyi hora homo in eis sedeat, quoniam sanae non sunt.
text_translation: Water, indeed, which are good for drinking, are good for baths; but they should be warmed moderately, and thus a man may sit in them daily, if he wishes, because they do not cause inflammation to man but give him a good and beautiful color. But waters which are bad for drinking are also bad for baths. If it is necessary, however, for someone to take baths in these, they should be boiled strongly, so that the liquid which is in them may be reduced by boiling, and a man should sit in them for a short time, because they are not healthy.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Sed et aquae pluyiamm aliquantum acres et acutae sunt, quod nubes et aer pluyiam de diyersis fluminibus bonis et malis et de humore terrae sursum trahunt, et ideo sanae non sunt. £t aquae istae per aerem yelut lexiya per cinerem descendunt, unde etiam acres et acutae efflciuntur; et si calefiunt, ita quod homo babiea in eis accipere yult, acumine suo cutem eius S transeunt et eum aliquantum laedunt. Aquae quoque niyium aliquantum turbidae sunt, et si quis homo bahiea in ipsis accepit, malos humores et scabiem inde sibi forte attrahit, quoniam aquae niyinm de squamis elementorum et frigoris et de sorde terrae snnt.
text_translation: But also the waters of rain are somewhat sharp and acute, because clouds and air draw rain from various rivers, good and bad, and from the moisture of the earth upwards, and therefore they are not healthy. And these waters, like lexic through ashes, descend, whence also they are rendered sharp and acute; and if they are heated, so that a man wishes to receive them, their sharpness passes through his skin and somewhat harms him. Waters of snow are also somewhat turbid, and if a man wishes to receive them, he may strongly attract bad humors and scabies from them, because waters of snow are from scales of elements and cold and from the dirt of the earth.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Aquae cisternamm ad balnea aliquantum leniores et utiliores sunt aquis pluviarum et niyium, quod aliquantum purificatae sunt. Si qui in aestate in fluentibus fluminibus balnea accipiunt, inde non laeduntur, quod fiumina ita calore solis et aeris temperata sunt, quod nec nimis calida nec nimis frigida sed temperata sunt. Sed tamen noxios et malos humores non multum compescunt, nec etiam ipsi mali humores per ea crescunt. De purgatoriis poenis, Quidam etiam inextinguibiles ignes in aere sunt, qui de diversis operibus hominum incenduntur, quoniam qui iUis ad gloriam esse debebant, ex malis operibus eorum ipsis ad poenales ignes fiunt, et ita in aliqua loca terrarum descendunt.
text_translation: The waters of cisterns for baths are somewhat milder and more useful than the waters of rain and snow, because they are somewhat purified. If anyone in summer takes baths in flowing rivers, they are not harmed, because rivers are thus tempered by the heat of the sun and air, so that they are neither too hot nor too cold but tempered. However, nevertheless, noxious and bad humors are not greatly restrained, nor do the bad humors themselves grow through them. Concerning purgatorial punishments, Some inextinguible fires are also in the air, which are ignited from diverse works of humans, because those who were meant to be in them for glory, through their own evil works, become themselves into punitive fires, and thus descend into certain places on earth.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Et ibi congregantur, ubi etiam aliqua flumina oriuntur et effluunt, quae calorem et ardorem ex eisdem ignibus contrahunt, ita quod etiam iudicio dei quaedam animae in ipsis ignibus et in ipsis aquis examinantur. Sed quidam rivuli ex eisdem aquis aliquando in diversas terras inter homines fiuunt, qui semper calidi sunt, quod de inextinguibilibus ignibus exeunt. Sed et quaedam partes terrarum sunt, super quas deus divina ultione aliquando ignis descendit, ut scriptum est: pluit super eos carbones ignis, et spiritus procellarum pars calicis eorum.
text_translation: And there they gather, where also certain rivers arise and flow out, which take heat and burning from those same fires, so that even by the judgment of God certain souls are examined in those very fires and in those very waters. But certain streams from those same waters sometimes flow into various lands among humans, which are always hot, because they issue from inextinguishable fires. But also certain parts of the earth are, upon which God sometimes sends fire by divine punishment, as it is written: "It rained upon them coals of fire, and the portion of the chalice of their storm spirit."

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Et terra et montes et lapides, quos ignis ille tetigit, in igne semper ardebunt usque ad novissimum diem, atque in illis locis, qui sic ardent, rivuli interdum oriuntur, qui de eodem igne semper calidi sunt et calidi fiuunt. Sed et homines interdum arte sua ad eadem loca aliquando quosdam rivulos ducebant, ut ex eis calefierent, qui per eadem loca fluentes et ardorem ibi accipientes deinde calidi effiuebant. Et aquae istae homines illos, qui balneis in eis utuntur, interdum non laedunt, sed eis sanitatem conferunt, quod calor earum iniustum ardorem, qui in illis est, compescit et malos humores in eis consumit.
text_translation: And the earth and mountains and stones, which that fire touched, will always burn in fire until the last day, and in those places, which thus burn, streams sometimes arise, which are always hot from the same fire and become hot. But also men sometimes by their own art lead certain streams to those same places, so that they might warm themselves from them, which flowing through those places and receiving heat there afterwards flow out as hot. And those waters, which men use in baths, sometimes do not harm, but confer health upon them, because their heat restrains the unjust heat which is in them and consumes the bad humors in them.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: De asso balneo, Homini, qui macer et aridns est, assum balneum, scilicet ignitis lapidibus calefactum, non yalet, quod per illad plns aridum se faoit. Illi autem, qni pingues cames habet, assum balneum bonnm et ntile est, qnoniam humores, qui in ipso snperflui sunt, per illud compescit et minuit. Sed et illi, qui uirgicMich est, balnea cnm ignitis lapidibus prosunt, quod humores, qui in ipso semper insurgunt, per assum balneum aliquantum comprimuntur.
text_translation: On the bath of ashes: To a man who is lean and dry, taking a bath, namely one heated by hot stones, is not beneficial, because through it he becomes more dry. To those, however, who have a fat body, taking a bath is good and useful, because the humors which are superfluous in him are restrained and diminished through it. But also those who are virgins benefit from baths with hot stones, because the humors which in them always rise up are somewhat compressed through taking the bath.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Sed de balneo aquarum insurgere et saUre in iniustum modnm aliquantum incipiunt, quoniam caro et sanguis ac venae illorum, qui uirgichdich sunt, in in stabilitatem diffundnntur, id est zeuUeint, Lapides autem ignem et diversos humores in se habent. Et si in ignem ponnntur, humiditas in eis perfecte auferri non potest, et ideo non est sanabile assum balneum cum eis facere, sed mnlto salubrius est cum lateribus, quod ipsi cocti et aridi sunt, ita quod humores, qui in eis erant, per coctionem ignis in ipsis consumpti et ablati sunt.
text_translation: But from the bath of waters rising and being healthy to an unjust measure somewhat begin, because flesh and blood and veins of those who are virgineis are spread into instability, that is, they are heated. Stones, however, have fire and various humors within themselves. And if they are placed into fire, humidity cannot be perfectly removed from them, and therefore it is not possible to take a bath with them, but much more healthful is with sides, which are cooked and dry, so that humors which were in them are consumed and removed by the cooking of fire in them.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Unde qui asso balneo lavari vult, illnd lateribns faciat; quod si lateres habere non potest, tunc griesteine accipiat, qnod illi suaviorem ignem et suaviores humores in se habent quam alii 2olapides; sed waclcun non accipiat, quod illi fortem ignem in se habent et quod diversis humoribus in aqua repleti snnt. De oculorum diversitate, Qui griseos oculos habet, interdum levis, interdum praeceps aut valde lascivns aut tardus aut in moribus suis incompositus est, sed tamen omnia, quae facit, probiter perficit.
text_translation: Wherefore, he who wishes to wash in a certain bath, let him do it on the sides; if he cannot have the sides, then let him take griseum, which has a milder fire and milder humores within it than other lapides; but calcinum should not be taken, because they have a strong fire within them and are filled with various humores in water. Regarding the diversity of eyes, he who has griseos oculos is sometimes levis, sometimes praeceps or very lascivus or tardus or uncomposited in his moribus, but nevertheless all things which he does, he completes probiter.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Qui igneos oculos habet, qui similes sunt nigrae nubi inxta solem sitae, iste pmdens et acutus ingenio ac iracnndus est. Item de octdis, Qui oculos habet similes nubi, ubi iris apparet, hic aliquantum instabilis est, videlicet interdum tristis, interdnm laetus, sed tamen in moribus suis honestns est. Item, Qni oculos habet similes turbidae nubi nec ad plenum igneae nec ad plenum turbidae, sed quae ali quantum glauca est, iste instabilis et levis in moribus suis est et astutus, sed tamen capax in operibus, ita quod quaeque opera nescit, facile capit et retinet.
text_translation: Who has fiery eyes, which are similar to a black cloud situated near the sun, he is prudent and acute in nature and irascible. Item of the eighth, who has eyes similar to a cloud, where the iris appears, this one is somewhat unstable, namely sometimes sad, sometimes joyful, but nevertheless honorable in his manners. Item, who has eyes similar to a turbid cloud, neither fully fiery nor fully turbid, but which is somewhat glaucous, this one is unstable and light in his manners and cunning, but nevertheless capable in works, so that whatever work he does not know, he easily learns and retains.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Item, Qni nigros seu torbilentos oculos habet, ut aliquando nubes est, iste prudens ac bonis consiliis consentiens est, sed tamen ad quaeque opera sua angustiatur. De certms et crapula ipsarum. Si quis cerasa come- dit, statim satis de vino bibat, ut succus earum per vinum superetur, et ita ille de eodem succo non laeditur. Quaedam de conceptu, Homines, qui concipiuntur, cum luna multas effdsiones aquarum in se portat in imbribus, aqua eos in subtnersionem ad se libenter trahit.
text_translation: Item, Qui nigros seu turbidos oculos habet, ut aliquando nubes est, iste prudens ac bonis consiliis consentiens est, sed tamen ad quaeque opera sua angustiatur. De certis et crapula ipsarum. Si quis cereasa comedit, statim satis de vino bibat, ut succus earum per vinum superetur, et ita ille de eodem succo non laeditur. Quaedam de conceptu, Homines, qui concipiuntur, cum luna multas effusiones aquarum in se portat in imbribus, aqua eos in submersio ad se libenter trahit.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: lo Et qui concipiuntur, cum luna in nimio ardore aestatis est, illos ignis ad se libenter trahit in combustione. Sed et cum dies caniculares sunt, qui tunc concipiuntur, quod mordaces dies sunt, illi a bestiis libenter devorantur. Et qui in loubroz concipiuntur, libenter de arboribus et aliis his altitudinibus ruunt. Luna prima, Sed homo, qui in prima luna concipitur, cum ipsa de sole splendorem suum recipit, si masculus est, superbus et durus erit nec uUum hominem amat, nisi illum, qui eum timet et honorat, ac homines libenter uerreihet et superbiam eorum et omnia, quae possident; sed sanus in corpore est nec magnas infirmitates habebit, sed non multum senex erit.
text_translation: And those who are conceived when the moon is in the excessive heat of summer, the fire eagerly draws them into combustion. But also when the canicular days are, those who are conceived then, because they are biting days, they are eagerly devoured by beasts. And those who are conceived in the moon's [lunae], eagerly fall from trees and other heights. The first moon, But the man who is conceived in the first moon, when it receives its splendor from the sun, if he is male, will be proud and harsh and will not love any man except him who fears and honors him, and he eagerly wages war and takes pride in their things and all that they possess; but he is healthy in body and will not have great infirmities, but will not be very old.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Si vero femina est, honorari semper appetifc et ab extraneis plus quam a domesticis semper amatur et apud se ipsam impia est et aUenos et noYos adventantes homines semper amat, sed circa domesticos mala est et illos negligit; et in corpore sana est. Sed si infirmitas eam apprehenderit, tunc valde infirmatur, et fere usque ad mortem, et diu non viyit. Luna secunda. Qai in secunda luna condpitur, si masculus est, vagationem mentis et in sapientia latitudinem mentis habet, et stabiles mores habet et ab hominibus reverenter honoratur, sed in timore facile exterretur et saepe sed leniter infirmatur et diutius vivere potest quam ille, qui in prima luna concipitur.
text_translation: If indeed she is a woman, she always desires honor and is loved more by strangers than by her own people, and within herself she is impious, and she always loves strangers and new arrivals, but concerning her own people she is evil and neglects them; and in the body she is healthy. But if weakness has seized her, then she is very weakened, and nearly to death, and does not live long. The second moon. He who is conceived in the second moon, if he is male, has a wandering of the mind and a breadth of mind in wisdom, and has stable manners and is honored by men with reverence, but is easily frightened in fear and often is gently weakened and can live longer than he who is conceived in the first moon.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Si autem femina est, pmdens erit et multa perquirit, et arbeitsam mores et sibi et aliis hominibus habet et amari vult, sed amari non HILDE6ARDIS potest, et per melancoliam fatigatur et suaiimtdi(^ facile est; sed diu viyere potest. Ltma tertia, Qui in tertia Inna concipitur, si masculus est, probus erit, et eadem probitas illi non prodest, quod alienos mores habet, et plus ad alienas causas quam ad suas habet et plus ad alienos homines quam ad notos anhelat et deum absque operibus diligit et in superbia sua facile deficit; sed facile in corpore infirmatur; sed tamen diu yivere potest.
text_translation: If, however, she is a woman, she will be prudent and seek much, and have industrious manners toward herself and other men, and she wishes to be loved, but cannot be loved by Hildegardis, and is tired by melancholy, and easily becomes of her own kind; yet she can live long. The third type, which is conceived in the third Inna, if male, will be good, and the same goodness does not profit him, because he has alien manners, and more inclines to alien causes than to his own, and more yearns for aliens than for known men, and loves God without works, and easily falls into pride; yet he easily becomes weak in body; yet he can live long.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Si vero femina est, infelici tatem et infortunium missesich in saecularibus habet, sed tamen suspiria ad deum tenet, et venae eius frequentes infirmitates patiuntur, sed tamen tolerabiles; et etiam diu vivere potest. Luna quarta, Qui in quarta luna concipitur, si mas culus est, fatuosus erit et facile per alios decipitur; sed tamen benevolus est et felicitatem habet, ita quod in superbia dives erit et quod honoratur, et in corpore sanus est et satis diu vivet, sed tamen non multum diu vivet.
text_translation: If, however, the woman is, she has sent misfortune and adversity into secular matters, but still holds sighs toward God, and her veins frequently suffer infirmities, but they are tolerable; and she can even live long. The fourth moon, he who is conceived in the fourth moon, if the male is, will be foolish and easily deceived by others; but still is benevolent and has happiness, so that in pride he will be rich and honored, and in body healthy and will live sufficiently long, but still not very long.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Si autem femina est, lobesam erit et hominibus cara et cum hominibus prospere incedit et facile infirmatur et defectum in corpore suo saepe sentit et diu non vivet. Ltma quinta, Qui in quinta luna concipitur, si masculus est, probus et fidelis, fortis ac durus erit et sanus in corpore et aliquamdiu vivet. Si autem femina est, virilis et stridich et nidich erit sed tamen proba , et interdum inforti infirmitate fatigatur, sed tamen non frequenter, et etiam aliquamdiu vivere potest.
text_translation: If, however, it is a woman, she will be fertile and beloved by people and will walk prosperously among people, and easily becomes weak and often feels a defect in her body and will not live long. Luna quinta, who is conceived in the fifth moon, if male, will be virtuous and faithful, strong and hardy, and healthy in body and will live for some time. If, however, it is a woman, she will be virile and stridic and nidic, but still virtuous, and occasionally fatigued by an inforti infirmity, but not frequently, and even live for some time.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Luna sexta, Qui in sexta luna concipitur, si masculus est, benevolus et blandus erit, sed viriles mores non habet et quasi femina mollis erit et facile infirmatur, sed diu non vivet. Si vero femina est, proba et frumich erit et cara hominibus, et sana in corpore est, et diu non vivet. Luna septima, Qui in septima luna concipitur, si masculus est, fatuosus et inanis sapientia erit, sed putat se sapientem esse, cum sapiens non sit, et hominibus amabilis non erit, et fortes venas habet nec multum infirmus in corpore est; sed si infirmitas eum apprehendit, letsam et suarCAU8AB ET LIB.
text_translation: The sixth moon: He who is conceived in the sixth moon, if he is male, will be benevolent and mild, but will not have masculine manners and will be soft like a woman and easily weakened, but will not live long. If, however, he is female, she will be trustworthy and fertile and beloved by people, and will be healthy in body, but will not live long. The seventh moon: He who is conceived in the seventh moon, if he is male, will be foolish and empty of wisdom, but will think himself wise, although he is not wise, and will not be beloved by people, and has strong veins and is not very weak in body; but if illness befalls him, letsam et suarCAU8AB ET LIB.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: V. mudich erit et aliquamdia vivet. Si autem femina est, frech erit, sed fatuosa et inanis sapientia et gamudich erit et taediosa hominibus et sana in corpore et diu vivet. Luna octava. Qni in octava lona concipitur, si masculus est, pmdens et castus et moderatus in omnibus operibus suis erit et adiutor hominum et in corpore sanus; sed si interdum infirmatnr, facile oonvalescet et satis senex erit, sed tamen non nimis senex. Si vero femina est, mmnedich et nithsam et zirgerne ac frumesam erit et viros non deligit et in corpore sana est et satis senex lO erit, sed tamen non nimis senex.
text_translation: V. mendich erit et aliquamdiu vivet. Si autem femina est, frequens erit, sed fatuosa et inanis sapientia et garrulus erit et taediosa hominibus et sana in corpore et diu vivet. Luna octava. Qui in octava luna concipitur, si masculus est, prudens et castus et moderatus in omnibus operibus suis erit et adiutor hominum et in corpore sanus; sed si interdum infirmatur, facile convalescet et satis senex erit, sed tamen non nimis senex. Si vero femina est, mendich et nitidus et zirgerne ac frumesam erit et viros non deligit et in corpore sana est et satis senex erit, sed tamen non nimis senex.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Ltma nona, Qui in nona luna concipitur, si masculus est, facile timet et incestus erit et infirmus in corpore, nec diu vivet. Si autem femina est, pudibunda erit et viros oum disciplina amabit et infirma in corpore erit nec diu vivet. Luna decima. Qui in decima luna concipitur, probus et frumesam ac utilis erit et felix et sanus in corpore, et diu vivet. Si vero femina est, proba erit et cara hominibus et nithsam ut lilium et frumesam ac felix, et facile infirmatur, sed oito convalescet et diu vivet.
text_translation: Luna nona. Qui in nona luna concipitur, si masculus est, facile timet et inconstans erit et infirmus in corpore, nec diu vivet. Si autem femina est, pudibunda erit et viros cum disciplina amabit et infirma in corpore erit nec diu vivet. Luna decima. Qui in decima luna concipitur, probus et frugiferum ac utilis erit et felix et sanus in corpore, et diu vivet. Si vero femina est, proba erit et cara hominibus et nitidam ut lilium et frugiferam ac felix, et facile infirmatur, sed octo convalescet et diu vivet.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Ltma undedma. Qui in undecima luna concipitur, si masculus est, iracundus erit et non felix et feminas non amat nec sanus erit in corpore nec multum diu vivet. Si autem femina est, heiemudich et arbeitselidt et wartselich in infamia erit, sed tamen proba; et magna infirmitate fadle apprehenditur, sed cito convalescet et non mnltum diu vivet. Luna duodecima. Qui in duodecima luna concipitur, si mascnlus est, indiscretus erit et mentem hac et illac divisam habebit et ad aliena loca et ad alienas res tendit et hoc diligit, atque omnes gestus eius molesti sunt hominibus, et suarsuhthicJi erit nec diu vivet.
text_translation: Luna undecima. Qui in undecima luna concipitur, si masculus est, iracundus erit et non felix et feminas non amat nec sanus erit in corpore nec multum diu vivet. Si autem femina est, hebetudine et laboriosus et vulgaris in infamia erit, sed tamen proba; et magna infirmitate facile apprehenditur, sed cito convalescet et non multum diu vivet. Luna duodecima. Qui in duodecima luna concipitur, si masculus est, indiscretus erit et mentem hac et illac divisam habebit et ad aliena loca et ad alienas res tendit et hoc diligit, atque omnes gestus eius molesti sunt hominibus, et suas subiectas erit nec diu vivet.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Si vero femina est, instabilis moribus erit et ungewieun nec doctrina meliorari vult et hominibus molesta erit et multas infirmitates non habebit, sed tamen non diu vivet. Luna tertia decima. Qui in tertia decima luna concipitar, si masculus est, grim erit et timidus et infidelis et homines libenter tserrethei et facile freneticus erit et diu vivet. Si autem femina est, minnesam non erit sed astuta et bonum consilium per deceptionem dat et paralysi fadle fatigabitur et diu vivet.
text_translation: If indeed the woman is, she will be unstable in morals and unwise, nor does she wish to improve her doctrine, and she will be burdensome to men and will not have many infirmities, yet nevertheless she will not live long. The thirteenth moon. Whoever conceives in the thirteenth moon, if male, will be grim and timid and faithless and will be willing to be deceived by men and will be easily frenzied and will live long. If however she is female, she will not have minnesang but will be cunning and will give good counsel through deception and will be fatigued by paralysis from falling and will live long.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Luna quarta decima, Qui in quarta decima luna concipitur, si masculus est, volmudich et superbus erit et laboriosam vitam habebit et honorem suum cum labore ad mortem ducet nec plurimos pueros de se generabit; et facile infirmatur, sed cito convalesoet, nec diu vivet. Si vero femina est, otmudich erit nec ulli honorem affectat, cum ipsa in honore sit, sed tamen honorem suum indefectum habebit et arJmtsam erit nec amari poterit, et sana in corpore sed diu non vivet.
text_translation: The fourteenth moon. Whoever is conceived in the fourteenth moon, if male, will be common and proud, and will have a laborious life, and his honor will lead him to death with labor, and he will not generate many children from himself; and he is easily weakened, but quickly recovers, and does not live long. If indeed a female, she will be inactive and does not seek any honor, since she herself is in honor, but nevertheless her honor will be unbroken, and she will be dry and cannot be loved, and healthy in body but will not live long.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Luna quinta decima, Qui in quinta decima luna concipitur, si masculus est, in honore et in felicitate erit et in omnibus operibus suis, quae inceperit, frumesam erit et in eis non deficiet, seu bona seu mala sinfc, quod in plenitudine lunae conceptus est, et sanus in corpore est et non diu vivet. Si autem femina est, kbesam evit et in novis operibus Itistich, et honorabitur et in his, quae ad deum sunt, facile peribit, si honorem deo non dederit, et facile infirmatur in corpore, sed tamen cito convalescet, et diu non vivet.
text_translation: The fifteenth moon, Whoever is conceived in the fifteenth moon, if male, will be in honor and in felicity and in all his works, which he begins, frumesam will be and in them he will not fail, whether good or bad sint, which are conceived in the fullness of the moon, and he will be healthy in the body and will not live long. If, however, it is a female, she will avoid having and in new works Itistich, and she will be honored and in those things which are towards God, she will easily perish, if she does not give honor to God, and she will easily become weak in the body, but nevertheless she will quickly recover, and she will not live long.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Luna sexta decima. Qui in sexta decima luna concipitur, si masculus est, viles mores habebit, qui nulH placebunt, sed squalidi erunt, et in operibus suis defectum habebit, sed tamen tantum superbiae habebit, quod in hac vita absque penuria sustentari poterit, et infirmitates facile non faligant eum , et senex erit. Si vero femina est, fatua erit et fatigabiles mores habebit, sed tamen sustentationem vitae in superbia habebit et in corpore sana est et diu vivet.
text_translation: The sixteenth moon. He who is conceived in the sixteenth moon, if he is male, will have vile manners, who will please no one, but will be wretched, and in his works he will have a defect, yet he will have pride alone, so that in this life he may be sustained without poverty, and infirmities easily do not weary him, and he will be old. If, however, she is female, she will be foolish and have fatigable manners, yet she will have sustenance of life in pride, and in body she is healthy and will live long.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Lu^a septima decima, Qui in septima decima luna concipitur, si mascuius est, alwere erit et in sapientia defectum habebit, sed tamen alio modo utilis est, et homines de eo ut de puero delectantur, et amabitur et in meduUa facile infirmabitur et non multum diu yivet, sed tamen satis senex erii Si autem femina est, stulta erit et siridich et iracunda, sed interdum benivola, ita quod ex hoc amabitur, et saepe cum asinnegemo giche fatigatur et etiam non multum diu vivet, sed tamen satis senex erit.
text_translation: Luna septima decima, Qui in septima decima luna concipitur, si masculus est, aliter erit et in sapientia defectum habebit, sed tamen alio modo utilis est, et homines de eo ut de puero delectantur, et amabitur et in medulla facile infirmabitur et non multum diu vivit, sed tamen satis senex erit. Si autem femina est, stulta erit et siridich et iracunda, sed interdum benivola, ita quod ex hoc amabitur, et saepe cum asin negemo giche fatigatur et etiam non multum diu vivet, sed tamen satis senex erit.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Luna octava decima. Qui in octava decima luna concipitur, si masculus est, fur erit et cupiditatem furandi habet et ita reperietur fur, et proprietas terrae ei abstrahitnr, ita quod fere nihil proprium de terra quaerit habere, scilicet nec agros nec vineas nec similia, sed lO semper aliis auferre, quae sua non sunt; et in corpore sanus est et per se diu vivet. Si vero femina est, astuta erit et vulpinos mores habebit et nihil fere loquitur, sicut in corde habet, sed propter nequitiam morum suorum in loquela sua homines decipit et probos homines ad mortem perducit, si poterit, et sana est in corpore, sed per insaniam interdum fatigatur et diu per se vivere potest, sed isti mores tam masculi quam feminae deo molesti sunt.
text_translation: The eighteenth moon. He who is conceived in the eighteenth moon, if male, will be a thief and possess the desire to steal, and thus the thief will be found, and the property of the earth is abstracted from him, so that he scarcely seeks to have anything proper from the earth, namely neither fields nor vineyards nor similar things, but always takes from others what is not theirs; and in body he is healthy and by himself will live long. If indeed female, she will be cunning and have fox-like manners and scarcely speaks, as she has in her heart, but because of the wickedness of her ways, by her speech she deceives men and leads good men to death, if she can, and in body she is healthy, but by insanity she is sometimes fatigued and can live long by herself, but these manners are troublesome to God both for males and females.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Ltma nona dedma, Qui in nona decima luna concipitur, si masculus est, simplex erit et nob astutus, sed amabilis hominibus, et superbia rerum ei deficiet, nisi per alios homines iuvetur, et sanus in corpore est, sed tamen non diu vivet. Si autem femina est, stulta erit et tamen amabilis hominibus, et in superbia sua facile deficiet, nisi ab aliis succurratur. Et facile infirmabitur, sed cito con- valescet, et non diu vivet. Vicesima. Qui in vicesima luna concipitur, si masculus est, virilis erit et malus et latro et homicida, et his delectatur et non facile infirmatur; sed si infirmitas eum apprehenderit, graviter infirmatur et non diu vivet.
text_translation: Luna nona decima. He who is conceived in the ninth and tenth moon, if male, will be simple and not cunning, yet lovable by men, and his pride in things will fail, unless he is aided by others, and he will be healthy in body, yet will not live long. If, however, it is female, she will be foolish yet lovable by men, and her pride will easily fail, unless others come to her aid. And she will easily become weak, yet quickly recover, and will not live long. Vicesima. He who is conceived in the twentieth moon, if male, will be virile and evil and a thief and murderer, and delights in these things and does not easily become weak; but if weakness seizes him, he will be gravely weakened and will not live long.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Si vero femina est, uerretheren et cedenseren erit et venefica et hominibus libenter tiergihet, et facile lunatica erit et diu vivet. Vicesima prima, Qui in vicesima prima luna concipitur, si masculus est, inanis mente et sensu erit et omni ^ tristitia plenus, et in nulla causa scit sibi providere, sed est ut stultus, qui in via errat; et iste in se ipso arescit et tristis est nec consolari potest; et rectas infirmitates non habebit, sed de tristitia mentis suae interdmn infirmatur, et sic diu yivet.
text_translation: If, however, she is a woman, she will be one who urinates and yields, and a witch, and gladly caresses men, and easily prone to lunacy, and will live long. The 21st, He who is conceived in the 21st moon, if male, will have a mind and sense empty, and full of every sorrow, and knows not how to provide for himself in any cause, but is like a fool who wanders in the road; and this one withers in himself and is sad and cannot be consoled; and he will not have proper infirmities, but from the sorrow of his own mind, he is weakened, and thus lives long.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Si autem femina Sst, ab aliis hominibus amabitur, sed timida et sollicita erit nec se ipsam corri gere scit, ita ut fere timendo periret, si etiam puer ei minaretur; et non multum in corpore infirmatur, sed de tristitia in mente gravatur et ita interdum debilitatur; et sic diu vivet. Vicesima secunda, Qui in vicesima secunda luna concipitui', si masculus est, duplex in mente erit et in bellis velut ille, qui in bello fortiter non pugnat; sed secundum hoc, quod ibi in prosperitate videt et secundum quod in prosperitate cognoscit, ita se ad homines habet; et ut aura mutatur, sic mens eius est; sed tamen ali quantum probus est, sed non multum amabilis hominibus, et in corpore sanus est et diu vivet.
text_translation: If, however, a woman is situated, she will be loved by other men, but she will be timid and anxious, and she does not know how to correct herself, so that she would almost die from fear, if even a boy threatened her; and she is not greatly weakened in the body, but she is burdened in the mind with sadness, and thus sometimes becomes weakened; and thus she will live long. Twenty-second, He who conceives in the twenty-second moon, if he is male, will have a dual mind and in battles will be like one who does not fight bravely in battle; but according to what he sees in prosperity and according to what he recognizes in prosperity, thus he behaves toward men; and as the wind changes, so is his mind; but nevertheless he is as much virtuous as he is, but not very lovable by men, and his body is healthy and he will live long.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Si vero femina est, inanis et vacua in moribus suis est et in eisdem moribus suis homines sibi attrahit et non in fomicatione, et libenter mentitur, et facile graves infirmitates incurrit, ut infirmitates sunt freneticorum et eorum, quorum cames vermes come dunt, et diu vivet. Yicesima tertia. Qui in vicesima tertia luna concipitur, si masculus est, benevolus erit et suavem mentem habebit et ad bonum consilium libenter se declinat et contra mala nescit in aliis astutias devitare et a felicitat« non deficit, sed tamen felicitatem vix retinet; et in corpore facile infirmatur, sed cito convalescet et diu vivet.
text_translation: If, however, she is a woman, she is empty and void in her morals, and in the same morals she attracts men not through fornication, and willingly lies, and easily falls into serious illnesses, such as the illnesses of the frenzied and those whose flesh is eaten by worms, and she will live long. Twenty-third. He who is conceived in the twenty-third moon, if male, will be benevolent and have a gentle mind, and eagerly turns himself toward good counsel, but does not know how to avoid cunning in others, and does not lack happiness, yet barely retains happiness; and in the body easily becomes weak, but quickly recovers and lives long.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Si autem femina est, pudibunda erit et in omnibus amabilis propter pudorem, quem habet; sed in superbia ratsam non est nec astuta, sed tamen aliquantum felix et non multum infirma in corpore, et diu vivet. Vicesima quarta. Qui in vicesima quarta luna concipitur, si masculus est, nachretich erit et ratsam et semper studet, ut apud se solum dives sit, et parcus et fere nulli homini prodest, et infirmitas corporis eum non multum laedit et satis diu vivet.
text_translation: If, however, she is a woman, she will be modest and lovable in all things because of the modesty she has; but in pride, she is not rash nor cunning, yet still somewhat happy and not very weak in body, and will live long. Twenty-fourth. He who is conceived in the twenty-fourth moon, if male, will be nachretich and rash and will always strive to be rich only among himself, and poor and scarcely benefits any man, and the weakness of the body does not greatly harm him and he will live sufficiently long.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Si vero femina est, prudens et listich erit et hominibus frumesam apparet, sed tamen eis non prodest; et non maltum in&rma in corpore est, sed tamen dragnnculam interdum patietur, et satis diu viyet. Vicesima quinta. Qui in vicesima quinta luna concipitur, si masculus est, stolis et freuel erit, et prudens fieret, si stoheiih et freueleit, quae in eo sunt, prudentiam in ipso (non dispergunt, velut ventus, qui pulverem dispergit. Et sic iste est, qui plus vult, quam habeat, scilicet ut navis, quae vix in aqua haeret; et in superbia saepe deficit et hominibus molestus est, et facile taediosas infirmitates habebit et diu non vivet.
text_translation: If indeed she is a woman, she will be prudent and lustful, and to men she appears as a wheat-ear, yet she does not benefit them; and although no evil is in the body, she will sometimes suffer from a dracunculus and live sufficiently long. Twenty-fifth. He who is conceived in the twenty-fifth moon, if he is male, will be foolish and furious, and if he is stolid and furious, those things which are in him will not disperse, as the wind which scatters dust. And thus he is one who desires more than he possesses, namely like a ship which barely clings to the water; and in pride he often fails and is troublesome to men, and easily suffers tedious infirmities and will not live long.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Si autem femina est, pulchra facie erit et erigit se quasi in probitate, et proba non est, quod cum probitas in ea quaeritur, non invenietur; et tunc odio habetur et in superbia deficiet; et non multum in corpore infirma est, sed non diu vivet. Vicesima sexta, Qui in vicesima sexta luna con^ cipitur, si mascnlus est, prudens erit et post quaeque opera prudenter cogitat et facile a febribus fatigatur et diu vivere potest. Si vero femina est, prudens erit et soUicita et stabilis et casta, sed a flegmate facile fatigatur et diu vivet.
text_translation: If, however, it is a woman, she will have a beautiful face and will raise herself as if in probity, but probity is not present, for when probity is sought in her, it will not be found; and then she is hated and will fail in pride; and she is not very weak in the body, but she will not live long. Twenty-sixth, He who is conceived in the twenty-sixth lunar phase, if male, will be prudent and after each work will prudently consider and is easily fatigued by fevers and can live long. If, however, it is a woman, she will be prudent and anxious and stable and chaste, but is easily fatigued by phlegm and will live long.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Vicesima septima. Qui in vicesima septima luna con- 2o cipitur, si masculus est, pavidus erit et facile ueregeseth se et ei etiam facile gruwet^ sed tamen probus est et utilis, et ab hominibus amatur, sed magnae pestes velut melancolia eum facile fatigant, et satis diu vivet. Si autem femina est, virtuosa erit, ita quod ab hominibus amabitur, et in corpore infirma erit, sed tamen satis diu vivet. Vicesima octava, Qui in vicesima octava luna concipitur, si masculus est, tortuosus in sensu et in moribus et in gestu suo erit et facit, quasi vergouchet sit, sed tamen satis de sensu et de scientia ad nomen suum habet nec amari potest, et pestem amentiae facile incurrit et satis diu vivet.
text_translation: Vicesima septima. He who is conceived in the twenty-seventh moon, if he is male, will be timid and easily flee from himself and also easily grow, but nevertheless he is good and useful, and is loved by men, but great plagues, such as melancholia, easily fatigue him, and he will live sufficiently long. If, however, she is female, she will be virtuous, so that she will be loved by men, and in body she will be weak, but nevertheless she will live sufficiently long. Vicesima octava. He who is conceived in the twenty-eighth moon, if he is male, will be twisted in sense and in morals and in his gestures, and he will act as if he were bent, but nevertheless he has sufficient sense and knowledge for his name and cannot be loved, and he easily incurs the plague of madness and will live sufficiently long.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Si vero femina est, fatua et stulta erit et fatigabiles mores habebit nec ab hominibus amari potest, et facile de febre fatigatur et satis senex erit. Vicesima nona, Qui in vicesima nona luna concipitur, si masculus est, curiosus erit et geumndene mores et gestus habebit et novos mores in vestitu et in moribus regionum Hildegardis oausae et curae ed. Kaiser. diligit atque novos et instabiles homines diligit, et in corpore yenenosos liiimores facile habet et ita saepe infirmatur et non diu vivet.
text_translation: If, however, she is a woman, she will be foolish and stupid and will have fatigable manners and cannot be loved by men, and she easily becomes fatigued by fever and will be sufficiently old. Twenty-ninth, He who is conceived in the twenty-ninth lunar phase, if he is male, will be curious and will have innate manners and gestures and new manners in clothing and in the customs of regions; Hildegardis oausae et curae ed. Kaiser. loves and loves new and unstable men, and in the body easily has venomous humors and thus often becomes ill and will not live long.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Si autem femina est, gemech et vana erit et in moribus et in gestn suo post se viros seducit, et in stomacho facile infirmatur et diu non vivet. Luna tricesima. Qui in tricesima luna concipitur, si masculus est, pauper erit, et si nobilis est, semper ad inferiora descendet nec felicitatem habebit, et in corpore, viribus et came facile deficit, sed satis diu vivet. Si vero femina est, pauper erit et wartseUch in infamia, et cum alienis hominibus libentius est quam cum notis; et non multum infirma in corpore erit et satis diu vivet.
text_translation: If, however, a woman is, twin and vain she will be, and in morals and in her gesture after herself she seduces men, and in the stomach easily becomes weak and will not live long. The 30th moon. Whoever is conceived in the 30th moon, if male, will be poor, and if noble, will always descend to lower things and will not have happiness, and in the body, strength and flesh easily fail, but will live long enough. If, however, a woman is, she will be poor and have a wart in infamy, and she prefers to be with strangers rather than with known people; and she will not be very weak in the body and will live long enough.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Qtiartm plagarum herhae quihus curis conveniant. Sanis et repente infirmantibus herbis in oriente nascentibus succurrendum est, melancolicis et latere dolentibus herbis in occidente nascentibus subveniendum est, paralysi et fortibus febribus cotidianis dolentibus, tertianis, quartanis et in stomacho dolentibus herbis in austro nascentibus subveniendum est, freneticis et in iecore dolentibus herbis in aquilone nascentibus succurrendum est. De conceptionis diversitate.
text_translation: Quartum plagarum herbae quibus curis conveniant. Sanis et repente infirmantibus herbis in oriente nascentibus succurrendum est, melancolicis et latere dolentibus herbis in occidente nascentibus subveniendum est, paralysi et fortibus febribus cotidianis dolentibus, tertianis, quartanis et in stomacho dolentibus herbis in austro nascentibus subveniendum est, freneticis et in iecore dolentibus herbis in aquilone nascentibus succurrendum est. De conceptionis diversitate.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Qui de nigro sanguine nascitur, puer cito incedit pedibus et cito loquitur, sed stultus est et difficiles mores habet, et si infirmabitur, diu languescet, quod niger sanguis livosus est et pestuosus. Infans, qui de rubeo sanguine nascitur, citius incedit et citius loquitur quam ille de nigro sanguine, et velox in cursu est et repentinus in moribus suis et cito in illis cessat et non diu languet, sed cito convalescit et prudens erit. Ru^eus sanguis santts.
text_translation: He who is born of black blood, the child quickly walks on his feet and quickly speaks, but he is foolish and has difficult manners, and if he becomes weak, he will languish for a long time, because black blood is livid and pestilential. The child who is born of red blood walks more quickly and speaks more quickly than he who is born of black blood, and is swift in running and sudden in his manners, and quickly ceases in them and does not languish for long, but quickly recovers and will be prudent. Red blood santts.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Infans, qui de spisso et recte colorato sanguine nascitur, pinguis in corpore erit et cito incedet, si prae mole corpore posset; et sanus est, sed et manibus cito reptat et non diu languet et prudens erit et utilis, et sanguis iste sanus est. Infans, qui de aquoso et tenui sanguine nascitur, vacuus in scientia est etlaborare non potest et facile infirmabitur nec facile irascitnr. ' et sanguis iste livosus est, et infans iste diu est, quod noQ incedit pedibus, et quod non loquitur, diu est.
text_translation: The infant who is born from thick and properly colored blood will be plump in body and will walk quickly, if able by the size of the body; and he is healthy, but also will quickly crawl with the hands and will not be long sick and will be prudent and useful, and this blood is healthy. The infant who is born from watery and thin blood is empty in knowledge and cannot labor and will easily become weak and will not easily become angry. And this blood is livid, and this infant is long-lasting, which does not walk on feet, and which does not speak, is long-lasting.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: Qmre homines dormUant post potionem et mintUionem, Quod homines post sumptas potiones seu post factas minutiones dormitant, hoc ex inauitione venarum evenit, quod cum venae inanitionem humorum et sanguinis in se sentiunt, quiescere volunt et ita homo repentinum soporem arripit. Contra coryzam, Si reuma a naribus superflue effluit, fumus de ligno abietis naribus excipiatur, et fluor ille lo suavius solvetur et cessabit. Cineres quoque de ligno abietis fiant et cum illis lexiva paretur et caput hominis cum illa lavetur, et pravos humores capitis minuit ac oculos clarificat.
text_translation: Quam homines dormiant post potionem et minutionem, quod homines post sumptas potiones seu post factas minutiones dormiant, hoc ex inanitione venarum evenit, quod cum venae inanitionem humorum et sanguinis in se sentiunt, quiescere volunt et ita homo repentinum soporem arripit. Contra coryzam, si rheuma a naribus superflue effluit, fumus de ligno abietis naribus excipiatur, et fluor ille lo suavius solvetur et cessabit. Cineres quoque de ligno abietis fiant et cum illis lexiva paretur et caput hominis cum illa lavetur, et pravos humores capitis minuit ac oculos clarificat.

source_text: Causae et Curae (Hildegard von Bingen), ed. Kaiser 1903
text: De febrivm causis, Febres autem de nimia dormitione aut de superfluis cibis et potibus oriuntur aut de taedio et otiositate, cum homo non operatur. Expliciunt prophetiae sanctae Hildegardis. Explicitur iste liber, scriptor sit crimine liber. Amen dicant omnia
text_translation: On the causes of fevers. Fevers arise from excessive sleep, or from superfluous foods and drinks, or from weariness and idleness, when man does not work. The prophecies of the holy Hildegard explain. This book is concluded; may the author be free from crime. Let all say Amen.
